claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know their age, and if you're claiming for multiple children, tell me each of their ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hi, thanks for helping me with this! I've got one child I'm asking about - Alex is 15 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
You're welcome! Happy to help you with this.

Great news - since Alex is 15 years old (under 16), they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me continue with the next step of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex working **less than 24 hours per week** in paid employment (or not working at all)?

This is just to confirm they're not in full-time work, which could affect eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at all - just focused on school at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's great to know. Since Alex isn't working at all, they clearly meet this requirement. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **not receiving** any of the following benefits in their own name?
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, is Alex free from claiming these benefits themselves? (Most 15-year-olds wouldn't be receiving these, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently on an apprenticeship?

- **No** - Alex is not in any apprenticeship
- **Yes** - Alex is in an apprenticeship (please let me know which type/region if so)

For reference, most apprenticeships would disqualify a child from Child Benefit, with the exception of **Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales** which are still approved.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex hasn't started an apprenticeship - they're still at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's clear - since Alex is still at school and not in any apprenticeship, that's no problem at all. Let me continue with the next part of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet **any one** of the following residency requirements?

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You usually reside in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **Pre-settled status and job seeking** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, option 1 (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next, I need to confirm you're responsible for Alex.**

Do **any** of the following apply to you?

1. **Living with Alex** - Alex lives with you in your home
2. **Contributing to Alex's upkeep** - You pay at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments for Alex under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that confirms you're responsible for Alex. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question about other claimants:**

Is anyone else currently claiming (or planning to claim) Child Benefit for Alex?

- **No** - No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- **Yes, but we've agreed I should claim** - Someone else was claiming but they've agreed to transfer it to you
- **Yes, and there's a dispute** - There's a disagreement about who should receive the Child Benefit

Which applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that keeps things straightforward. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next, I need to check Alex's living situation:**

Where is Alex currently living?

- **At home with you (normal living)** - Alex lives at home in a regular family setting
- **In hospital** - Alex is currently staying in hospital
- **In local authority care** - Alex is in care provided by the local authority
- **In a fostering arrangement** - Alex is being fostered

Which best describes Alex's situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm fostering Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for letting me know you're fostering Alex. Let me check the rules around fostering arrangements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question about your fostering arrangement:**

Is the local council **NOT** paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

In other words:
- **Yes** - The council is not contributing to Alex's accommodation or maintenance costs (you may be eligible)
- **No** - The council IS paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance (this would affect eligibility)

This is an important distinction for fostering arrangements and Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - I don't get anything from the council to foster Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. Since the council isn't paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance, let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: You've already confirmed the council isn't paying towards Alex's care, so let me proceed to the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful news! Based on the information you've provided, I can now give you my assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Great News - You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit for Alex!

Based on everything you've told me, you meet all the eligibility requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

### Summary of Your Situation:
| Requirement | Status |
|------------|--------|
| Child's age (under 16) | ✅ Alex is 15 |
| Child not working full-time | ✅ Alex is at school |
| Child not receiving own benefits | ✅ Confirmed |
| Not in disqualifying apprenticeship | ✅ Not applicable |
| You live in the UK | ✅ Manchester |
| You're responsible for the child | ✅ Alex lives with you |
| No other claimant | ✅ Confirmed |
| Fostering arrangement | ✅ Council not paying |

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit Charge
5. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receive certain benefits)

### Useful Link:
👉 [How to Claim Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

reasonings: Alex meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Age: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16 and automatically qualifies. (2) Not working: Alex is not in paid employment. (3) No benefits: Alex does not receive ESA or Universal Credit in their own name. (4) No apprenticeship: Alex is in school, not on an apprenticeship. (5) Claimant residency: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester). (6) Responsibility: Alex lives with the claimant. (7) No other claimant: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. (8) Fostering arrangement: Although Alex is fostered, the local council is NOT paying towards accommodation or maintenance, which means Child Benefit can still be claimed.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fostering Alex (aged 15) without receiving any financial support from the local council. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, not working, not receiving their own benefits, not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, the claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for the child, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The fostering arrangement does not disqualify them because the council is not contributing to Alex's care costs.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You can claim Child Benefit for a fostered child if the council is not paying for their accommodation or maintenance

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

(2) Not working: Alex is not in paid employment. (3) No benefits: Alex does not receive ESA or Universal Credit in their own name. (4) No apprenticeship: Alex is in school, not on an apprenticeship. (5) Claimant residency: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester). (6) Responsibility: Alex lives with the claimant. (7) No other claimant: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. (8) Fostering arrangement: Although Alex is fostered, the local council is NOT paying towards accommodation or maintenance, which means Child Benefit can still be claimed.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fostering Alex (aged 15) without receiving any financial support from the local council. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, not working, not receiving their own benefits, not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, the claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for the child, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The fostering arrangement does not disqualify them because the council is not contributing to Alex's care costs.
